# Codex Toolbox: Agent Setup Notes

This repository contains a Node.js bridge that mirrors Codex app-server threads into Telegram forum topics or Discord project channels. Use this file when an AI agent needs to install, configure, run, or debug the app in a fresh Codex instance.

Repository URL: https://github.com/yhdesai/codex-toolbox

## What The App Does

- Connects to Codex app-server over JSON-RPC.
- Polls Codex threads and maps each active/new thread to one Telegram forum topic or one Discord text channel.
- Sends human-readable Codex messages into Telegram or Discord.
- Routes Telegram/Discord replies from allowlisted users back into the mapped Codex thread.
- Posts Codex approval requests with inline approve/decline/cancel buttons.
- Tails Codex CLI session JSONL files for CLI-created sessions that do not stream live app-server item events to this bridge process.

## Files To Read First

- `README.md`: end-user setup and command reference.
- `src/index.js`: environment configuration and app entrypoint wiring.
- `src/bridge.js`: main orchestration, Telegram commands, topic mapping, CLI file tailing, mirroring policy calls.
- `src/discord-bridge.js`: Discord orchestration, project category/channel mapping, commands, and approval callbacks.
- `src/codex-app-server.js`: Codex app-server JSON-RPC client wrapper.
- `src/telegram.js`: Telegram Bot API wrapper.
- `src/discord.js`: Discord REST and Gateway wrapper.
- `src/state.js`: local JSON state file.
- `test/bridge.test.js`: expected command and mirroring behavior.
- `test/discord-bridge.test.js`: expected Discord command and mirroring behavior.

## Required Runtime Inputs

Never invent, print, or commit real secrets. Ask the user to provide these through environment variables or their process manager:

```sh
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me
TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<telegram-user-id>[,<telegram-user-id>]
```

For Discord:

```sh
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me
DISCORD_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<discord-user-id>[,<discord-user-id>]
CODEX_SYNC_PROVIDER=discord
```

Optional environment:

```sh
CODEX_APP_SERVER_COMMAND=codex
CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS="app-server proxy"
CODEX_APP_SERVER_CWD=/path/to/workspace
CODEX_TOOLBOX_STATE=~/.codex-toolbox.json
CODEX_TELEGRAM_POLL_MS=5000
DISCORD_PROJECT_NAME=workspace-folder-name
DISCORD_COMMAND_PREFIX=!codex
```

If `codex app-server proxy` does not initialize, retry with:

```sh
CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS="app-server"
```

## Fresh Setup Procedure

1. Verify Node.js and Codex CLI:

```sh
node --version
codex --version
```

2. Install dependencies:

```sh
npm install
```

3. Run tests:

```sh
npm test
```

4. Start locally for Telegram:

```sh
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me \
TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<telegram-user-id> \
npm start
```

For Discord:

```sh
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me \
DISCORD_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<discord-user-id> \
CODEX_SYNC_PROVIDER=discord \
npm start
```

5. In the Telegram forum group, send:

```text
/bind
```

6. Create or discover a thread:

```text
/new Test thread
```

To start a thread in a specific directory:

```text
/new --cwd /absolute/path/to/project Test thread
```

Then send a Telegram reply inside the new topic and confirm it reaches Codex.

For Discord, invite the bot to the server with Message Content Intent enabled and permissions for `Manage Channels`, `View Channels`, `Send Messages`, and `Read Message History`. Run:

```text
!codex bind
!codex new Test thread
```

Then send a Discord message inside the created channel and confirm it reaches Codex.

## PM2 Setup

Use PM2 for a persistent background bridge:

```sh
npm install -g pm2

TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me \
TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<telegram-user-id> \
CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS="app-server proxy" \
pm2 start bin/codex-toolbox.js --name codex-toolbox --update-env

pm2 save
```

Restart after env or code changes:

```sh
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me \
TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<telegram-user-id> \
CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS="app-server proxy" \
pm2 restart codex-toolbox --update-env

pm2 save
```

Discord PM2 example:

```sh
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me \
DISCORD_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<discord-user-id> \
CODEX_SYNC_PROVIDER=discord \
DISCORD_PROJECT_NAME=codex-toolbox \
CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS="app-server proxy" \
pm2 start bin/codex-toolbox.js --name codex-toolbox-discord --update-env

pm2 save
```

## Telegram Requirements

- Destination must be a supergroup with forum topics enabled.
- Bot must be admin.
- Bot needs permission to create/manage topics and send messages.
- Group is bound at runtime with `/bind`; do not hardcode the group id.

## Discord Requirements

- Destination must be a Discord server where the bot is installed.
- Enable Message Content Intent in the Discord Developer Portal.
- Bot needs `Manage Channels`, `View Channels`, `Send Messages`, and `Read Message History`.
- Server is bound at runtime with `!codex bind`; do not hardcode the guild id.
- The project category defaults to the workspace folder name, or `DISCORD_PROJECT_NAME`.

## Command Reference

- `/bind`: bind current forum group.
- `/help`: list commands.
- `/new Optional title`: create Codex thread and Telegram topic.
- `/new --cwd /absolute/path Optional title`: create Codex thread and Telegram topic rooted in a specific directory. `--dir` is accepted as an alias. Paths must be absolute; `~/path` is supported.
- `/topics`: list mappings.
- `/delete_all_topics confirm`: delete Codex-mapped Telegram topics and clear mappings.
- `/unlink`: remove current topic mapping without deleting the topic.
- `/relink <threadId>`: map current topic to an existing Codex thread.
- `/resync`: force thread discovery.
- `/pause`: pause Codex-to-Telegram mirroring only.
- `/resume`: resume mirroring and discover threads.
- `/rename <title>`: rename current topic and attempt Codex thread rename.
- `/interrupt`: interrupt current mapped Codex thread.
- `/status`: show diagnostics.
- `/logs`: show redacted diagnostics and PM2 log tails.

Discord commands:

- `!codex bind`: bind current Discord server and create/reuse the project category.
- `!codex help`: list commands.
- `!codex new Optional title`: create Codex thread and Discord channel.
- `!codex status`: show diagnostics.
- `!codex resync`: force thread discovery.
- `!codex interrupt`: interrupt current mapped Codex thread.
- `!codex unlink`: remove current channel mapping without deleting the channel.
- `!codex relink <threadId>`: map current channel to an existing Codex thread.
- `!codex delete_all_channels confirm`: delete Codex-mapped Discord channels and clear mappings.

## Public Repo Safety

Before committing or pushing, run:

```sh
rg -n "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN='|DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN='|[0-9]{6,}:[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}|/home/|boundChatId|-100[0-9]+" .
npm test
```

Do not commit:

- Real Telegram bot tokens.
- Real Discord bot tokens.
- Real Telegram user ids unless the user explicitly wants examples containing them.
- Real Discord user ids unless the user explicitly wants examples containing them.
- `~/.codex-toolbox.json`.
- PM2 logs.
- Codex session JSONL files.
- Local absolute paths from a user's machine.

Use placeholders in docs:

```sh
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me
TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<telegram-user-id>
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=replace-me
DISCORD_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=<discord-user-id>
```

## Important Implementation Details

- Default allowlists are empty. If `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS` or `DISCORD_ALLOWED_USER_IDS` is not set, no chat user can control that bridge.
- Unauthorized normal Telegram text is ignored silently.
- Unauthorized approval callbacks are answered with a denial callback message.
- Discord non-`bind` commands are accepted only in the bound server.
- Discord maps each project to a category and each Codex thread to a text channel under that category.
- Telegram-originated messages are suppresssed when app-server emits the corresponding Codex user message, to avoid echoing user input back into the topic.
- Assistant deltas are buffered until `item/completed`.
- Existing mapped CLI sessions start file tailing at EOF to avoid backfilling old history.
- Newly discovered CLI sessions start file tailing at byte 0 so the first CLI prompt and answer can appear in the newly created topic.
- `/delete_all_topics confirm` can only delete topics known in local bridge state; Telegram bots cannot enumerate arbitrary forum topics.
- Telegram `/new --cwd` validates that the requested directory exists before calling Codex `thread/start` with `cwd`.

## Useful Diagnostics

```sh
pm2 list
pm2 logs codex-toolbox --lines 120 --nostream
sed -n '1,220p' ~/.codex-toolbox.json
npm test
```

Existing installs may still use the legacy `codex-telegram-topic-sync` binary name or `CODEX_TELEGRAM_STATE`; both remain supported for compatibility.

Probe app-server direct stdio:

```sh
node -e "const {spawn}=require('child_process'); const cp=spawn('codex',['app-server'],{stdio:['pipe','pipe','pipe']}); cp.stdout.on('data',d=>console.log(d.toString())); cp.stderr.on('data',d=>console.error(d.toString())); cp.stdin.write(JSON.stringify({id:1,method:'initialize',params:{clientInfo:{name:'probe',title:null,version:'0'},capabilities:{experimentalApi:true}}})+'\\n'); setTimeout(()=>cp.kill(),5000)"
```

## Common Fixes

- Topic creation denied: make bot a Telegram group admin with topic management permissions, then run `/bind` again.
- App-server proxy timeout: run with `CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS="app-server"`.
- Messages from Telegram ignored: verify sender id is in `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS`.
- CLI messages not mirrored: run `/resync`, then check `thread/list` returns `path` for the CLI thread and that the session JSONL file is readable by the bridge process.
